pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 76 - Python code creates this file path
setup_task.sh line 57 - tickerSymbol element set to AMZN
setup_task.sh line 32 - glitch date is exactly 2023-07-14 with price 0.01
export_result.sh line 22 defines THRESHOLD=5000000000, which equals $50 USD (5000000000/100000000). This is the threshold for detecting outliers below acceptable price range.

Code analysis of quotes in setup_task.sh lines 23-44 shows actual minimum non-glitch price is $127.13 (2023-07-10). Real AMZN July 2023 data from StatMuse confirms prices range from $127.13 to $135.36. The metadata value of $120.00 is below the actual minimum price in the dataset.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetically generated Portfolio Performance XML data containing 20 historical AMZN quotes for July 2023. Real historical AMZN prices from StatMuse confirm the quoted data is accurate, with prices ranging from $127.13 to $135.36 for trading days in July 2023. The glitch (price 0.01 on 2023-07-14) is synthetically inserted below the threshold of $50.00 USD (internal value 5000000000). The security ticker is AMZN, and the target file is /home/ga/Documents/PortfolioData/amzn_glitch.xml. The metadata claim for expected_min_price ($120.00) is contradicted by the actual data minimum of $127.13.
